title: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet
wikitext:
[]: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Apr 16, 2018 2:56 pm I found a midi of Sakura Nagashi and decided to turn it into a String Quartet. This is a rough draft, I have not played Cello in several years, so any suggestions would be appreciated. https://musescore.com/user/28572003/scores/5072043 Credits to Maestro-kun for transcribing the music. Notes: 1-8 Couldn't figure out how to transcribe the intro since one hand is outside the natural range of any one instrument. I figure splitting it between the violin and viola was a good compromise. 9-28 Couldn't be bothered to figure out how to transcribe the rest of the piano into string instruments so I left this as a violin solo.

[]: Re: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Apr 17, 2018 1:41 pm I thought it would be an audio file. Shows how little I know about music I guess lol But this is great work for the community. I love Sakura Nagashi so much.

[]: Re: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Apr 26, 2018 8:13 pm kuribo-04 wrote: I thought it would be an audio file. Shows how little I know about music I guess lol But this is great work for the community. I love Sakura Nagashi so much.

[]: Re: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Apr 27, 2018 5:12 am I'll check it out. :)

Article: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet
[]: Sakura Nagashi for String Quartet             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Apr 16, 2018 2:56 pm I found a midi of Sakura Nagashi and decided to turn it into a String Quartet. This is a rough draft, I have not played Cello in several years, so any suggestions would be appreciated. https://musescore.com/user/28572003/scores/5072043 Credits to Maestro-kun for transcribing the music. Notes: 1-8 Couldn't figure out how to transcribe the intro since one hand is outside the natural range of any one instrument. I figure splitting it between the violin and viola was a good compromise. 9-28 Couldn't be bothered to figure out how to transcribe the rest of the piano into string instruments so I left this as a violin solo.
